identity.name: AWS IAM Access Key Abuse — Discovery + S3 Object Exfiltration
identity.summary: An attacker obtains a long-lived AWS IAM access key (via leaked .env file, exposed CI artifact, or developer endpoint compromise), enumerates the account from an attacker-controlled host using GetCallerIdentity, ListBuckets, and ListUsers, then exfiltrates S3 objects via GetObject from an unusual source IP and user-agent.
identity.description: Per Unit 42's 2025 Cloud Threat Research, IAM-centric techniques drive the majority of cloud incident investigations, and CSP-managed policies grant ~2.5× more permissions than customer-managed ones. This simulation reproduces the canonical 'leaked key → enumerate → exfil' chain that maps to the Cortex Cloud / CDR detection narrative: surface non-human identity behaving like a human, anomalous enumeration burst, and bulk object reads from outside the expected AWS service principal.

actors.name: Opportunistic credential abusers (TeamTNT, Scarlet Eel, automated scanners)

techniques.name: Exfiltration Over Web Service: Exfiltration to Cloud Storage

prerequisites.description: Lab AWS account with a sandboxed IAM user that has read-only S3 + IAM ListUsers permissions.
prerequisites.remediation: Provision the lab IAM user via the bundled Terraform module (terraform/aws-lab/iam-victim/).

prerequisites.description: Lab S3 bucket with marker objects.

input_variables.name: LEAKED_KEY_ID
input_variables.description: Lab IAM user access key ID.

input_variables.name: LEAKED_KEY_SECRET
input_variables.description: Lab IAM user secret access key.

input_variables.name: LAB_BUCKET
input_variables.description: Lab S3 bucket name pre-populated with marker objects.

expected_artifacts.description: Rapid sequence of CloudTrail events: GetCallerIdentity → ListUsers → ListRoles → ListBuckets from a new sourceIPAddress.
expected_artifacts.expected_value: eventName in (GetCallerIdentity, ListUsers, ListRoles, ListBuckets) within 60s

expected_artifacts.description: S3 ListObjects + bulk GetObject calls on the target bucket.
expected_artifacts.expected_value: eventName in (ListObjects, GetObject) AND requestParameters.bucketName = LAB_BUCKET

expected_artifacts.description: Non-AWS-service userAgent (e.g., aws-cli/2.x rather than Lambda/EC2 service principal) used by an IAM user not historically tied to that workload.

manual_steps: Rotate the lab access key after each POV run via `aws iam create-access-key` + delete prior.
Confirm no marker objects were modified (GetObject is read-only; nothing else should have changed).

biocs.name: IAM User Discovery Burst From New Source IP
biocs.description: ≥4 distinct AWS Discovery API calls (GetCallerIdentity, ListUsers, ListRoles, ListBuckets, DescribeInstances) within 60 seconds from a sourceIPAddress and userAgent combination not previously observed for that IAM user.
biocs.logic:
dataset = cloud_audit_logs
| filter cloud_provider = "AWS" and event_source in ("sts.amazonaws.com", "iam.amazonaws.com", "s3.amazonaws.com", "ec2.amazonaws.com")
| filter event_name in ("GetCallerIdentity","ListUsers","ListRoles","ListBuckets","DescribeInstances","ListAttachedUserPolicies")
| bin _time span=60s
| comp count_distinct(event_name) as distinct_calls by user_identity.arn, source_ip, user_agent, _time
| filter distinct_calls >= 4 and source_ip not in user_baseline.recent_ips[user_identity.arn]

biocs.name: S3 Bulk Object Download by IAM User With No Prior Read Pattern
biocs.description: An IAM user (not a service role) issues ≥50 GetObject calls against a single bucket within 5 minutes, when historical baseline shows <5/day.
biocs.logic:
dataset = cloud_audit_logs
| filter cloud_provider = "AWS" and event_source = "s3.amazonaws.com" and event_name = "GetObject"
| filter user_identity.type = "IAMUser"
| bin _time span=5m
| comp count() as get_count by user_identity.arn, request_parameters.bucketName, _time
| filter get_count >= 50 and get_count > 10 * baseline.daily_avg[user_identity.arn, request_parameters.bucketName]

biocs.name: Long-Lived IAM User Key Used From Outside Known ASN
biocs.description: Access key auth observed from an ASN never seen for this identity in 90 days.
biocs.logic:
dataset = cloud_audit_logs
| filter cloud_provider = "AWS" and additional_event_data.MFAUsed = "No"
| filter user_identity.type = "IAMUser"
| enrich asn = geoip(source_ip).asn
| filter asn not in user_baseline.asns_90d[user_identity.arn]

xql_queries.name: Confirm discovery burst within last 30 min
xql_queries.query:
dataset = cloud_audit_logs
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter cloud_provider = "AWS" and event_name in ("GetCallerIdentity","ListUsers","ListRoles","ListBuckets")
| fields _time, user_identity.arn, source_ip, user_agent, event_name
| sort _time desc

xql_queries.name: Confirm S3 GetObject burst on target bucket
xql_queries.query:
dataset = cloud_audit_logs
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter event_name = "GetObject" and request_parameters.bucketName = "${LAB_BUCKET}"
| comp count() as gets by user_identity.arn, source_ip

xql_queries.name: Find IAM users with both discovery burst and large S3 read within 1h
xql_queries.query:
dataset = cloud_audit_logs
| filter cloud_provider = "AWS"
| join (filter event_name in ("ListUsers","ListBuckets")) as disc on user_identity.arn within 1h
| join (filter event_name = "GetObject" | comp count() by user_identity.arn | filter count > 50) as exfil on user_identity.arn within 1h

correlation_rules.name: Cloud — IAM Key Compromise With Exfiltration
correlation_rules.logic: BIOC(IAM User Discovery Burst From New Source IP) AND BIOC(S3 Bulk Object Download by IAM User With No Prior Read Pattern) within 60m

products.evidence_query: Compliance: IAM least-privilege findings on the simulated user.
ASM: surface leaked-credential indicator if the access key matches a known exposure.
Playbook: Cloud IAM Key Compromise — disable key, rotate, notify owner, snapshot CloudTrail window for forensics.

use_cases.name: Leaked Cloud Credentials → Data Exfiltration Defense
use_cases.description: Detect and contain abuse of long-lived IAM access keys that surface outside their expected operating envelope, before bulk data egress completes.

test_cases.objective: Cortex Cloud ITDR raises an incident on the discovery burst within 10 minutes of CloudTrail event ingestion.
preconditions: AWS CloudTrail forwarding to Cortex Cloud is configured.
Baseline of ≥30 days of CloudTrail data for the lab account.
steps: Wait up to 10 minutes for ingestion + analytics.
success_criteria: Incident timeline includes the GetCallerIdentity/ListUsers/ListBuckets sequence.
Source IP + user-agent flagged as `new for identity`.

test_cases.objective: CDR detects the S3 bulk-read exfiltration pattern.
success_criteria: CDR alert `CDR-EXFIL-S3-001` raised with severity Critical.
Volume metric (object count + bytes) matches `aws s3 cp --recursive` summary.
Correlation rule `CR-CLOUD-0001` links discovery + exfil into one incident.

test_cases.objective: Prisma Cloud + ASM pre-existing posture findings reinforce the narrative.
success_criteria: Prisma Cloud `PC-IAM-KeyAge-90d` flagged the lab access key as over-aged.
If ASM is enabled and the key was seeded into a public test repo, ASM `ASM-LEAKED-KEY-001` surfaced it.

test_cases.objective: XSOAR automated response disables the compromised key.
success_criteria: Access key set to `Inactive` via IAM API within 5 minutes.
Slack/Teams notification posted to cloud-security channel.

preventive_controls: Eliminate long-lived IAM access keys in favor of short-lived STS sessions via IAM Identity Center / OIDC federation.
Enforce MFA on all IAM users; require MFA condition keys on sensitive S3 actions.
Apply S3 bucket policies that deny `*:GetObject` from non-VPC endpoints.
Use SCPs to deny IAM user creation in workload accounts; centralize identity.
detection_engineering: Baseline each IAM principal's source IP/ASN, user-agent, and API call cadence; alert on first-seen combinations.
Alert on GetObject volume anomalies per (principal, bucket) pair.
Hunt for IAM users authenticating without MFA from new ASNs.

references.title: IAM Your Defense Against Cloud Threats: Unit 42 Cloud Threat Research
Cloud Threats on the Rise: Alert Trends Show Intensified Attacker Focus on IAM, Exfiltration
Responding to Cloud Incidents — 2025 Unit 42 Global IR Report
Stratus Red Team — AWS Attack Techniques
MITRE ATT&CK T1530 — Data from Cloud Storage
